text: German midfielder Michael Ballack scored twice as Bayern Munich defeated Real Madrid 3-1 in the Champions League quarter-final at the Allianz Arena on Tuesday.
The United Nations Security Council voted unanimously to impose new sanctions on North Korea following its latest missile test over the Sea of Japan.
Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the company's new headquarters in Cupertino, California, describing it as the most environmentally sustainable building ever constructed.
The temperature dropped to minus fifteen degrees overnight with heavy snowfall expected to continue through the weekend across most of the region.
French President Emmanuel Macron met with British Prime Minister at the Elysee Palace in Paris to discuss Brexit trade negotiations and European security cooperation.
Researchers at Stanford University and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology published findings in Nature showing that the new CRISPR technique can edit multiple genes simultaneously.
The stock market closed higher today with gains across all major indices as investors responded positively to the latest employment figures.
Japanese automaker Toyota announced a joint venture with Tesla to develop next-generation electric vehicle batteries at a new facility in Nagoya.
Nobel Prize laureate Malala Yousafzai addressed the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, calling for increased investment in girls' education across South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa.
The recipe calls for two cups of flour, one tablespoon of baking powder, a pinch of salt, and three large eggs mixed together until smooth.
